Looking at the image, the left green square moved from inside the left frame to the right! It's now at the right edge of the left gray area. So clicking on the right frame border selected the left green square for movement, and RIGHT moved it.
Wait, that's strange. Let me re-examine. Previously the left green was at rows 33-35, cols 12-14. Now looking at the image, the left green square appears to be at roughly rows 33-35, cols 18-20 (moved right by about 6 columns, or 2 moves of 3 columns each).
Actually wait, I only pressed RIGHT once. Let me check the exact positions.
Actually, looking more carefully at the image, the left green square is now at the right edge of the left gray area, near the small horizontal connector. It moved from inside the frame to the right.
So the pattern seems to be: clicking on something selects a green square for movement, and arrow keys move it. But which green square gets selected depends on what you click.
Let me check the current positions and figure out the selection logic.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green: boundary={n['boundary']} children={n['children']}")
        for cid in n['children']:
            c = next(x for x in current_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if x['id'] == cid)
            print(f"  child: color={c['color']}")

# Check frames
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 16:
        print(f"\nFrame: boundary={n['boundary']} children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
